passage: SNpc dopaminergic neurons exhibit autonomous pacemaking driven by L-type calcium channels, creating chronic calcium stress

Dopamine itself is a source of oxidative stress through auto-oxidation and MAO-B metabolism
Mitochondrial Complex I deficiency is found in SNpc neurons of PD patients

Multiple PD genes (GBA1, LRRK2, ATP13A2) converge on lysosomal pH regulation and autophagy

Loss of PINK1 or Parkin disrupts mitochondrial quality control, leading to accumulation of damaged mitochondria

SOX6+/ALDH1A1- dopaminergic neurons in the ventral SNpc tier are most vulnerable in PD
